reviews.review_text: The toilets are in a dreadful state; the owner came to wake us up at 7am with the payment terminal in hand, even though it’s clearly stated at the entrance that payment must be made before 10am. There’s no kitchen; the barbecue costs $1 and only works for barely seven minutes. We had to pay $8 for a washing machine; this campsite is a disgrace and should be avoided. The Discovery opposite is much better, with much friendlier staff, a kitchen and washing machines for $4.

This campground has been extremely useful for me on numerable occasions and despite the decreptitude  in some areas I can afford it as a single, I can take my dog and best of all, you can light a fire. People should ask about places thoroughly I guess.
Great camping spot. Close to Byron
Receptionist was lovely, manager a bit less, but please clean the toilets mate

Price reasonable but manager of the place terrible!!
Treated me and my friend in a not friendly way from the start. Felt very uncomfortable around him. Looks like he hates his job and people/ campers.

The worst experience I have ever had in my 3 years of travels. The manager was vile speaking down on my girlfriend and her friend making them feel like they were worthless (all because of a broken watching machine that they asked for help with). He then done groundwork right next to our site without informing us getting our washing we was drying dusty and dirty. He then came over to our camper and pointed at myself asking me where I was staying and have I paid to be here even though he had seen me there for 3 days and asked me to extend that morning. We were made to feel so uncomfortable we decided to refund our stay and leave Byron bay but they then cancelled this the next morning without notifying us. The only positive of this place was the lovely young girl at reception who actually got in trouble for being a good person for trying to give us our refund (which they made very clear was a new staff member and had “no right to do so”). PLEASE SAVE YOUR MONEY DONT STAY HERE.

We were very skeptical about coming here due to the reviews but we had absolutely no issues, facilities were some of the best we’ve had travelling the east coast so far, clean etc. Pricey but it’s Byron Bay so can’t expect much else. Didn’t encounter the notorious Dave at any point.

Stayed here for four days just after new year 2026. It is a well equiped park with adequate facilities. The reception staff are super friendly and clearly enjoy what they do. It is the most cost effective park in Byron and I would definately stay here again.

I stayed here over newyears. The manager acts like a prison warden. He was rude from the get go and would constantly be driving around checking everyone. We were told to wind down at 10 pm on the 1of jan, our music was at a very reasonable volume. I also overheard him talking to a group of campers near us, he told them they were getting kicked out or had to pay 300 dollars extra to stay. These people were making hardly any noise. We were also charged $55 per person even though it was one vehicle and one swag plus a minimum 2 nights stay. The bathrooms are very run down with tiles missing everywhere. I don’t recommend camping here, there’s places with far better value for money not to mention it feels like being in a prison camp.
receptionist is a lovely lovely lady!!! don’t stay here if you want a nice place, but it’s good and fairly cheap for a stay
Workers carrying on at what looks like permanent residents making the park feel hostile and uncomfortable. Not a happy place. Amenities are super rundown. Workers not friendly.

Camping sympathique ! nous étions dans une cabine avec 5 personnes et nous avons passé un bon moment. Il est mieux d’avoir une voiture pour rejoindre le centre de Byron Bay mais reste tout de même accessible. Bon rapport qualité prix.
Pas de wifi ..

Unpleasant|I made a reservation for one person, indicating that someone would be joining me the next morning to pick up my car.|The agency and the manager charged me for two people, which was double the price.|I asked them to check the cameras to see that I was alone, but they refused.|The overall rating of 2.5 stars is understandable.|I do not recommend this place.

Unpleasant
I made a reservation for one person, indicating that someone would be joining me the next morning to pick up my car.
The agency and the manager charged me for two people, which was double the price.
I asked them to check the cameras to see that I was alone, but they refused.
The overall rating of 2.5 stars is understandable.
I do not recommend this place.
What a dump.

Worst experience ever. Definitely will not be back and hopefully people read the previous reviews and stay away not like me. The only good thing about this place was the beautiful reception lady. The manager has no idea how to run a business hence why it was the only caravan park that was near empty. No customer.service at all rude and arrogant. The way we were spoken to was rude. This place will be shut in no time if the manager keeps going this way. What a joke

THIS IS THE WORST CARVAN PARK I HAVE STAYED AT IN 3 MONTHS OF TOURING THE EAST COAST. STAFF ARE RUDE YOU CANT FEED THE BIRDS THERE IS NO WIFI AND THE AMENATIES ARE CRAP BEST THING THERE IS THE SUNRISE AND SUNSETS AND YOU CAN GET THEM ANYWHERE....

This is advertised as a holiday park but is more like a prison camp, mostly permanent residents who are unfriendly, strong south African presence. The managers and staff are hostile, rude and aggressive. Dave the manager will follow you around the park and interrogate you or any of your visitors! The receptionist has no manners or hospitality and commented on my relationship with my partner encouraging her to leave me while enquiring about activities in the area. Dogs can be at the park but are not welcome more like monitored and despised. Very strange place and environment! I have never in my life been treated or experienced such hostility at any holiday park apartment or hotel. By far the worst experience to date.

Site was good. Stayed 3 weeks - nice neighbours and good service by beck. She made it easy and good experience.
Location I excellent for Byron Bay and surroundings.
Update - second time around same review

Worst experience. Immediately, upon check in, was met with a rude lady demanding we put a sticker on the car (which is super hard to get off by the way) to prove your staying there or whatever. If your a visitor you have to pay for every 3 hours your there during the day. DO NOT just drive through like other hotels to go visit family or friends. You will be hunted down by an arrogant male manager, who is beyond rude and demanding. He must feel like a cop running that dump, even if you pay… you’re constantly being watched. He drives past your lot staring in at you. You feel like you can’t breathe, have space or just simply be on a holiday by the amount of pressure.

Have talked to people living there/staying there and they all can’t stand him. Don’t know how the place is still running. The lady in the reception, if you want to call it that anyways, tried to make out that my partner was being rude and has told him he has to leave by tomorrow. He wanted to pay for me to stay the night around 3pm and that I’m going to go to work really early in the morning. Kept asking irrelevant questions trying to get me to have to pay for my car to be there in the morning even though it was going to be around 4 am that I left. Way before reception was open. Then she asked if he was my boyfriend. I said yes. She said RUN. I said I’ve been with him for 7 years. She goes on to say “oh so you get it”. I just didn’t respond because I couldn’t believe how she was acting. She was saying all this personal stuff when she should have been professional. This was a grown woman.

I used to enjoy staying here before there were new owners. Who ever owns the dump clearly doesn’t care how the people are managing it. They just want your money but for the WORST service.
Absolutely disgusting behaviour, there’s too much to write about what else was wrong but yeah if you don’t believe me go and experience it yourself.
Women don’t stay here.
Nice camp-site for Bluesfest, clean showers and toilets and friendly staff. Dave the manager is great just respect the property and behave decently and you'll have a good experience

Great experience. The staff were super friendly and so helpful. It was a busy weekend and anytime we needed anything they were down to help us out in no time at all. Thanks for making our stay great and happy Easter 😊
Great venue for a conference

Absolutely disgraceful experience. This place is filthy, completely unhygienic and not maintained at all. The manager is extremely aggressive and threatening—he has no respect for people and uses intimidation to create fear. He constantly finds excuses to withhold your bond, clearly trying to profit off tenants dishonestly. It’s clear this behavior has been going on for years, and the worst part? The owners know and do nothing about it. They allow this toxic environment to continue without any accountability. I strongly advise you to stay far away from this place. There are much better and safer options out there.
Nous avons été parfaitement accueilli et le camping disposait de tout le nécessaire pour passer une nuit avec notre tente de toit ! Merci beaucoup 🙂
BE CAREFUL, THEY ARE SCAMMERS! THEY KEPT MY BOND OF 550$ FOR NO REASON! I CLEANED ALL FOR 2HOURS, GAVE THE ROOM BACK IN PERFECT CONDITION! OWNER IS A REAL SCAMMER BE AWARE

We had a 15mt set up , was awesome space to park , quite,  green , cheap and cheerful  money we saved on parking up we spent in town on wonderful food etc. Others staying were friendly, from different demographics and places around the world.
Disgusting attitude from staff! Extremely aggressive. Not sure if it even safe to stay there. You would need to be very careful if you stay here!!

Nice place. We’re were taken very fast and stayed only 2 nights. The amenities were very nice. And it was quit and peaceful. There was a nice view for the sunset which was beautiful. I would recommend this as a stopover as there ain’t much to do. The reception was so nice and gave us a late check out. I would also recommend to put a pool of some sort to elevate this place.

Moin Fans, dieser Campingplatz ist nicht zu empfehlen. Dreckige Toiletten und Duschen sind ganz oben auf der Serviceliste. Den Fusspilz kann man förmlich beim Duschen beim auf die Füße überspringen zusehen.
Des Weiteren ist das Personal am Empfang nicht hilfsbereit. Zu allem Überfluss ist der Preis dann auch noch utopisch hoch, sodass man verarmt.
Peace out.

can’t rate it anything above 1 star because some bloke that works here went full psycho on my mate and his girlfriend for where they had parked before they could pay (tried to park out of the way as there was no room in the car park with trailer on the back). It seems like a recurring problem based on the other reviews, he seems mentally inept for the job. Someone should get him some help, and obviously shouldn’t work with people.

My boyfriend and I have the best experience here.
We’ll definitely recommend this beautiful place to our friends.

This place was absolutely ‼️filthy ‼️I am a young girl at 19 years of age went to take a shower while all the men in workers We’re standing in the entry to the female showers. I have to get my mum to stand in the shower with me ‼️then went to use the bathroom and found a mail worker in the female bathrooms just playing on his phone. ‼️The laundry room are very filthy. The workers are Horrible, I haven’t had the chance to meet the manager Dave thank God as I will only be staying here for one night and will be gone in the morning. This place should be shut down. It’s not a safe place ‼️there are rats 🐀 ‼️

Meget slidt campingplads. Det er som de skriver, lav budget.
Der er en del fastliggere. Meget ligner Klondike.
Vi oplevede ikke ting der ikke virkede. Alt virkede ok rent, men gammelt og slidt.

Rude management, having to pay for terrible BBQs was very disappointing. Campsites covered in very prickly weeds that wouldn’t take that much effort to eradicate and properly maintain. I didn’t think it was possible to be so disappointed by a campsite.

I was instructed by a staff member(Fiona) to go to the office to ask the manager if we could extend our stay by one night using a  credit I had from a previous visit. When I got there, I was meet by an aggressive male (ground staff member) who was obviously expecting me. He stood over me and bluntly said "you can't use your credit." Then he got physical when I asked for a refund instead. The manager did nothing except turn a blind eye while I was getting shirt fronted. When I got this guy off, I took a step back to create some space between us, then he slammed the office doors CLOSED. From behind the locked glass doors this guy patronisingly gestures for me and my family to leave the grounds while the incompetent manager continued to ignore the situation. By this stage I was upset and responded with some brief gestures of my own before returning to my campsite just after dark. Shortly after, we got a drive by from the campgrounds white ute to make the whole experience even more uncomfortable. It goes without saying that our stay was rudely cut short and the manager did not honour our credit or provide a refund.

My feedback for staff: my 1 star review is being generous considering the poor treatment I received. In future, try being polite and tactful rather than aggressive and unreasonable. It can go a long way in your line of work.

Fortunately, we only needed the room for one night as our car had broken down on the highway at Batemans Bay.  It was disgusting, The first room I offered us, I lifted up the sheets and found several little flying insects in the bed, The second room they offered us had no heater air-conditioner or microwave and behind the tips on the hand basin was quite dirty so we didn’t even have a shower at the place.  We Stayed in seven motels over a two week period and it was the only one that wanted $100 bond and $150 a night for a very basic room.  $150 a night for an Off the highway motel Should have bought us a very comfortable room for the night.  If we weren’t so tired after breaking down, we would not have stayed there.  It was a real disappointment after having just spent several knots on North Stradbroke Island in superior accommodation.

I don’t understand the bad reviews, we stayed 3 weeks and had a great time. Ideal for solo travellers and couples. Quiet hours after 9pm, affordable prices 315$ per week powered site. No swimming pool. It has everything you need, nature and wildlife. Thanks guys for hosting us, we will definitely come back 💚

Belongil Fields is a lovely place to stay.  Surrounded by trees, wild life -  Staff are helpful and friendly. Place is kept clean. Close to shops, surfing beaches and walking distance to Byron Bay.  I met many friendly traveller's from overseas and everyone  enjoyed staying at Belongil Fields. This place is a gem and so relaxed, laid back and exactly what you need to chill & enjoy Byron Bay.
The manager is terrible and the cleaning staff too.
The campsite manager has been very disrespectful to my boyfriend and me. They have kicked us out for no reason from one day to the next. I don't recommend this place.
Great little spot, only affordable place in Byron.
Nice park

Manager is abusive and has used physical violence on a guest who was a minor-aged 17. Looking at previous reviews this appears to be the standard treatment of his guests. Would not recommend staying there.

Für eine Nacht in Byronn Bay geeignet. Länger oder mit Kindern wohl eher nicht.
Preise im Vergleich zu anderen Plätzen etwas zu hoch. (Woanders kriegt man fürs selbe Geld bessere Anlagen, pool usw.)

It’s up to 5 stars, isn‘t it?|So let’s start with 5 stars and check all we love in tourist parks:|• Cleanliness: given. Amenities were thoroughly cleaned. That’s fine.|• Location: well, it’s Byron Bay. A good location in itself. The campground is in a green area, not too far from the beach. Fine again.|• Amenities’ condition: well, they are a bit worn-out. A huge bit. The shower heads e.g. spray, but in many directions. Water hits you, but as well the ceiling, from where it slowly drippes back onto you … hmmm. In (at least one) of the showers there was a sign “do not mess with the shower heads, or they will be removed”. Interesting. The behaviour of the shower heads could be clearly identified of them just being old. And punishing later guests for any misbehaving of existing guests? Well, half a star has to be taken.|• Services available: no pool, a single small camp kitchen for a lot of lots. Do not even think of W-LAN or a decent shop. Deduction of half a star.|• Lots themselves: grass ground, a bit uneven, no concrete for table, chairs, … Another half star going away, unfortunately.|• Supplies: Power only on lot. No water or grey water gully. Sorry, other campgrounds offer that. Minus ½, so we are down to three stars.|• Costs: per person, per night. We paid 50 AUD per person per night, which is quite expensive for a campsite like this. Reducing by another ½.|• Management: awful. You can’t book in advance, but are told “just pass by before 5 p.m., there’s plenty of empty space”. Arriving right before 5 p.m. the lady in the office has to radio the manager to ask if there’s still a powered site available. He doesn’t answer. She phones. He answers, and we can stay. For two nights, please. No, that’s not possible to be granted right now. Come back to the office the other morning and ask again. Apologies, because management is mainly done by pen and paper. Handover of a sticker to be put onto the car, which indicates our allowance for this night, and this night only. No dedicated lot, just “find yourselves a lot in this row” (where a lot , a LOT of empty spots were available, even though obviously some long-term customers watering their little gardens use up some spots). Sorry, but this costs another half star, which makes two.|• Prolongation: the next morning at about 08:30 asking for the possibility to stay for a second night is considered “a bit early”. Seriously? If we’d had to leave we had to do it by 10 a.m. – and preparing for a leave is a bit different from a relaxed breakfast on site. In the end, we were allowed to stay, “handcuffed”, as we had to wear admittance bands on our wrists, and no, my husband couldn’t just take mine for me, I had to go to the office in person. The next sticker was handed over to us (pre-unwrapped as the other one, because it had to be stuck onto the vehicle IMMEDIATELY – causing my husband to walk back to the campervan with a sticky sticker on his hand, irritatedly shaking his head). Oh, that’s not good.|• Manager himself: what an experience. Throughout the prolongation process he entered the office, and, well, he wasn’t the friendliest. Most irritating moment was when he asked about the number of our lot, he then stated that there was no such number. Sorry, mate, you were given the exact number written on our power supply. But, yeah, that’s peanuts. So only ½ star deducted for all the really unfriendly and unwelcoming interactions.|Oh, now we are down to 1 star. It’s not possible to award less, so that’s it.|

It’s up to 5 stars, isn‘t it?
So let’s start with 5 stars and check all we love in tourist parks:
•	Cleanliness: given. Amenities were thoroughly cleaned. That’s fine.
•	Location: well, it’s Byron Bay. A good location in itself. The campground is in a green area, not too far from the beach. Fine again.
•	Amenities’ condition: well, they are a bit worn-out. A huge bit. The shower heads e.g. spray, but in many directions. Water hits you, but as well the ceiling, from where it slowly drippes back onto you … hmmm. In (at least one) of the showers there was a sign “do not mess with the shower heads, or they will be removed”. Interesting. The behaviour of the shower heads could be clearly identified of them just being old. And punishing later guests for any misbehaving of existing guests? Well, half a star has to be taken.
•	Services available: no pool, a single small camp kitchen for a lot of lots. Do not even think of W-LAN or a decent shop. Deduction of half a star.
•	Lots themselves: grass ground, a bit uneven, no concrete for table, chairs, … Another half star going away, unfortunately.
•	Supplies: Power only on lot. No water or grey water gully. Sorry, other campgrounds offer that. Minus ½, so we are down to three stars.
•	Costs: per person, per night. We paid 50 AUD per person per night, which is quite expensive for a campsite like this. Reducing by another ½.
•	Management: awful. You can’t book in advance, but are told “just pass by before 5 p.m., there’s plenty of empty space”. Arriving right before 5 p.m. the lady in the office has to radio the manager to ask if there’s still a powered site available. He doesn’t answer. She phones. He answers, and we can stay. For two nights, please. No, that’s not possible to be granted right now. Come back to the office the other morning and ask again. Apologies, because management is mainly done by pen and paper. Handover of a sticker to be put onto the car, which indicates our allowance for this night, and this night only. No dedicated lot, just “find yourselves a lot in this row” (where a lot , a LOT of empty spots were available, even though obviously some long-term customers watering their little gardens use up some spots). Sorry, but this costs another half star, which makes two.
•	Prolongation: the next morning at about 08:30 asking for the possibility to stay for a second night is considered “a bit early”. Seriously? If we’d had to leave we had to do it by 10 a.m. – and preparing for a leave is a bit different from a relaxed breakfast on site. In the end, we were allowed to stay, “handcuffed”, as we had to wear admittance bands on our wrists, and no, my husband couldn’t just take mine for me, I had to go to the office in person. The next sticker was handed over to us (pre-unwrapped as the other one, because it had to be stuck onto the vehicle IMMEDIATELY – causing my husband to walk back to the campervan with a sticky sticker on his hand, irritatedly shaking his head). Oh, that’s not good.
•	Manager himself: what an experience. Throughout the prolongation process he entered the office, and, well, he wasn’t the friendliest. Most irritating moment was when he asked about the number of our lot, he then stated that there was no such number. Sorry, mate, you were given the exact number written on our power supply. But, yeah, that’s peanuts. So only ½ star deducted for all the really unfriendly and unwelcoming interactions.
Oh, now we are down to 1 star. It’s not possible to award less, so that’s it.
Was met with rude staff who were on a massive power trip.

Checked in here and we were met with a rude staff member because it was close to closing time.

The camp sites are roped off and we were told to be quiet at 9pm when we weren’t close to any other campers, nor were we making much noise.

I’m in customer service and regardless of the customer, I always try to turn an experience around, but there was no attempt at that.

Can’t recommend this spot unless you’re just in need of a place to camp for the night.

I heard the owners want to sell the place, therefore the low maintenance and upgrades that should be carried out. This place could be of high potential.

The location is great, Emma from reception is very kind. And the prices make up for the value.
Horrible experience. Dave the manager is a genuinely terrible human being. One of the first truly terrible people I have personally come across in years. Stay away!!

Record your conversations so that you can threaten them with legal action if they try to steal your money. Better yet, just go somewhere else.

- They will try to steal your money if they think that they can get away with it.
- Several of the staff members are rude, belittling, overtly and unnecessarily aggressive… Just weird.

It would almost be comedic if it didn’t have the possibility of leaving you without a place to stay, while they pocket your money and kick you out, as per previous reviews.

What a bizarre place. Very strange. Not an experience that I’ll forget. Nevertheless, I suppose there was some good that came out of it. I discovered what a business full of immoral megalomaniacs looks like. I learned that you should be cautious of some small businesses and stand up to them if they are trying to take advantage of you.

Would recommend. 10/10. Stay here if you want to gain some perspective on how unnecessarily hostile and dishonest people can be.

Rude abusive angry power tripping ranger responded to my husband with blatant lies! Neither of us drink. There’s videos of him online telling people to leave with no good reason. This place should NOT be open.

Disgusting man, Dave that runs this place!!! He abuses people and uses his managerial authority to intimidate and bully people, especially women. He should NOT be allowed to run a caravan park where there are children and women staying!! He is creepy and dangerous!! Even his own wife is scared of him!!! He NEEDS to be SHUT DOWN!! Will NEVER go there again and will NEVER allow anyone I know to stay there!! Dave should be ashamed of himself!!

🚨Please read below before book anything to this insecure camping 🚨

‼️‼️Don't go to this place and run away before it's too late ! The manager is the most impolite and rude character I met in Australia ‼️‼️

Here the storie :

We were unpacking our stuff with my two friends on the two emplacements we had previously paid for one night. We removed gently the thin rope between the two emplacement to have more space to unpack.

The manager came to us in his ute and asked my in a really unfriendly way to put the rope back. After put the rope back I asked him why and I complained how rude he was. He asked us to leave straight way without any apparent reason and he opened my car without ask me the permission in order to remove the camping sticker. Bec the employe at the box office is on his side.

I call the cops and unfortunetly they can't do nothing. Me and my friends felt really insecure and I understand now why this place has a so low rate. Please don't give any dollars to this kind of human being. 🙏( I'm a french backpacker and that's the first time something like this happened to me in 3 years. 🤬)
Fair, peaceful and helpful staff. Well kept. Can recommend
